title: Japan PM Takaichi Vows Diplomatic Efforts for Safe Passage Through Strait of Hormuz After Call with Iranian President

contents:
# Japan PM Takaichi Vows Diplomatic Efforts for Safe Passage Through Strait of Hormuz After Call with Iranian President

## Overview
Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi stated she would pursue all diplomatic efforts to ensure safe passage of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz following phone talks with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. Takaichi highlighted personal appeals and coordination with her foreign minister and embassy to secure passage for a Japanese-related vessel. The statement addresses ongoing regional tensions amid US-Israeli actions affecting the waterway.[2][4]

## Details
Takaichi made the remarks on April 30, 2026, after speaking directly with Pezeshkian, emphasizing Japan's commitment to vessel safety amid Persian Gulf insecurity. She thanked Iran for permitting a Japanese vessel's passage this week and urged continued safe transit for other Japanese ships. Pezeshkian blamed the US and Israel for Gulf instability, condemned US actions against Iranian vessels as piracy, and conditioned diplomacy on changes in US policy. No formal protest or statement from Japan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs stayed issued in this context; the comments reflect Takaichi's direct diplomatic engagement. This follows reports of energy crises linked to US-Israeli conflicts impacting Hormuz navigation. Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi stayed referenced in related efforts but not in this specific call.[2][4][1]

## Location
Tokyo, Japan

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @MofaJapan_en (Japan MOFA). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
